title: Basic Threshold Check Algorithm
description: Algorithm demonstrating how capacity tracking tools check thresholds and generate alerts
code:
PROCEDURE CheckThreshold(metricValue, threshold, metricName)
BEGIN
 IF metricValue > threshold THEN
 alertLevel = CalculateAlertLevel(metricValue, threshold)
 alertMessage = "Alert: " + metricName + " exceeded threshold"
 alertMessage = alertMessage + ". Current: " + metricValue + ", Threshold: " + threshold
 GenerateAlert(alertLevel, alertMessage)
 LogAlert(alertMessage, metricName, metricValue, threshold)
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 ENDIF
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE CalculateAlertLevel(current, threshold)
BEGIN
 ratio = current / threshold
 IF ratio >= 1.5 THEN
 RETURN "CRITICAL"
 ELSE IF ratio >= 1.2 THEN
 RETURN "WARNING"
 ELSE
 RETURN "INFO"
 ENDIF
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This pseudocode shows the core logic of threshold-based alerting in capacity tracking tools. The algorithm compares current metric values against predefined thresholds and generates alerts with appropriate severity levels.

title: Data Aggregation for Historical Analysis
description: Algorithm for aggregating raw capacity data into time-based summaries
code:
PROCEDURE AggregateCapacityData(rawData, timeInterval)
BEGIN
 aggregatedData = EmptyMap
 FOR EACH dataPoint IN rawData DO
 timeKey = RoundToInterval(dataPoint.timestamp, timeInterval)
 IF timeKey NOT IN aggregatedData THEN
 aggregatedData[timeKey] = NewAggregationBucket()
 ENDIF
 aggregatedData[timeKey].AddMetric(dataPoint)
 END FOR
 
 summary = EmptyList
 FOR EACH bucket IN aggregatedData DO
 summaryRecord = NewRecord()
 summaryRecord.timestamp = bucket.startTime
 summaryRecord.avgCPU = bucket.Average("cpu_usage")
 summaryRecord.maxCPU = bucket.Maximum("cpu_usage")
 summaryRecord.avgMemory = bucket.Average("memory_usage")
 summaryRecord.maxMemory = bucket.Maximum("memory_usage")
 summaryRecord.totalDiskIO = bucket.Sum("disk_io")
 summaryRecord.totalNetwork = bucket.Sum("network_throughput")
 summary.Add(summaryRecord)
 END FOR
 
 RETURN summary
END PROCEDURE
explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how capacity tracking tools aggregate raw metric data into summarized records for efficient storage and historical analysis, computing averages, maximums, and totals.
